title: Ethical Transition, Rejection Learning, and Continuing Career Development · Independent Career Launch Board
summary: An unfamiliar candidate with nontraditional evidence, strict privacy and accessibility needs, limited runway, a changed local market, ambiguous listings, conflicting employer signals, a suspicious recruiter, role gaps, public-proof decisions, a tailored application, a reciprocal contact, a multistage interview, a disproportionate take-home request, jurisdiction-specific questions, competing offers, and an ethical transition must defend every claim and learner-owned decision. Planned artifact: P…
objectives: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.
Connect one demonstrated behavior to a changed role context without claiming unobserved expertise.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
